title: 🔴 The AI Coding Marketplace is Finally LIVE!
summary: Archon Workflow Marketplace launched to accept community-submitted AI coding workflows. The marketplace uses an auto-review pipeline built and operated by Archon itself to process submissions.
writeup: A live coding stream walking through the launch of Archon's agentic workflow marketplace, merging the first community-contributed workflow, the idea-to-work-order generator. The session shows end-to-end how community contributions work, how a GitHub Actions pipeline driven by Archon itself handles automated security review and auto-merge, and closes with a candid discussion of Anthropic's June 15 pricing change that ends subsidized SDK access for subscribers.
key_points: The idea-to-work-order workflow takes a raw feature idea, interviews the developer through a series of clarifying questions, and produces a set of fully specced phased work orders tied to the actual codebase, going beyond a PRD by diving into implementation detail for each phase.

Community contributions to the Archon marketplace follow a manifest-only pull request model: the author hosts the workflow in their own public repo, then opens a PR that updates a single manifest file in the Archon repo pointing to their commit SHA, keeping maintenance decentralized.

Archon drives its own marketplace governance: a GitHub Actions workflow installs Archon and runs a security review and analysis workflow on any PR that touches the manifest file, then auto-merges if the workflow passes, removing the burden from human maintainers entirely.

Anthropic announced that Claude agent SDK programmatic access, previously covered by a Max subscription, will require separate payment from June 15, with $200 per month in credits included; the streamer notes that $200 burns quickly with Opus and flags Codex and Pi as alternatives for ambient agentic workloads.
